---
description: "Use when creating visual assets, illustrations, icons, gradients, backgrounds, hero graphics, SVG artwork, OG images, favicons, or any graphic/visual design task. Covers composition, color theory, visual hierarchy, and aesthetic principles for digital products."
---

# Graphic Design

## Visual Hierarchy

- Establish clear focal points — the eye should know where to look first, second, third
- Use size contrast aggressively: hero headings 3–5× larger than body text
- Weight and color create hierarchy within same-size text: bold + primary vs regular + muted
- Group related elements with proximity; separate unrelated elements with whitespace
- Use alignment to create invisible lines that connect elements across the layout

## Composition

- Apply the rule of thirds for hero sections and key visual areas
- Use asymmetric layouts for dynamic, modern feel — perfect symmetry feels static
- Create visual rhythm through repetition of shapes, colors, and spacing patterns
- Lead the eye with directional cues: gradients, lines, arrows, gaze direction in photos
- Negative space is compositional — intentionally frame content with emptiness

## Color Theory

- Use 60-30-10 rule: 60% dominant (background), 30% secondary (surfaces), 10% accent (CTAs, highlights)
- Create depth with color: darker = recedes, lighter/brighter = advances
- Use color temperature intentionally: cool (blue, cyan) = trust, tech; warm (orange, amber) = energy, urgency
- Gradients should flow naturally: analogous colors (adjacent on wheel) for smooth gradients, avoid complementary pairs
- For dark UIs: never use pure black (#000) — use very dark blues/grays (#0a0a0f, #050710) for richness

## Icons & SVG

- Design on a consistent grid (24×24, 20×20, or 16×16)
- Use consistent stroke width across all icons in a set (1.5px or 2px)
- Maintain optical balance — some shapes need subtle size adjustments to appear equal
- SVG optimization: remove unnecessary groups, minimize path data, use `currentColor` for theming
- Prefer outline style icons for UI, filled for emphasis or active states

## Backgrounds & Textures

- Subtle noise textures (2–4% opacity) add premium tactile quality to flat surfaces
- Mesh gradients create organic, modern ambient effects
- Use radial gradients for spotlight/glow effects behind hero content
- Grain/noise + gradient combo: apply noise over gradient for depth without banding
- Glassmorphism: semi-transparent background + backdrop-blur + subtle border + shadow

## Hero & Marketing Graphics

- Hero sections need a single clear message — one headline, one CTA, one visual
- Use product screenshots/mockups at slight angles (2–5°) for dimension
- Ambient glow behind product shots creates focus and premium feel
- Browser/device frames add context and credibility to product screenshots
- Animated subtle elements (floating particles, gentle gradients, pulse effects) add life without distraction

## OG Images & Social Assets

- OG images: 1200×630px, keep text in safe zone (center 60%), large readable fonts
- Favicons: design at 32×32, test at 16×16 — simplify aggressively at small sizes
- Maintain brand consistency: same colors, fonts, and visual language across all assets
- Dark backgrounds work better for OG images — more visual impact in feeds
